methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Orchid is a Series A biotech company that has raised $16.5M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 companies—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms in the Biotech & Health sector at the same or adjacent stage, including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Chai Discovery, and Asimov.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $16.5M raised proxy, implying a $33M valuation consistent with typical Series A advancement. Peer-median valuation uses a cohort of 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies with a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied as Orchid's revenue is not disclosed, which is standard for private-stage companies; the sector median P/S of 6.7x across the three public comparables remains uninformative without this input.

The triangulated valuation range is $28M (low), $666M (median), to $1.5B (high). The wide spread reflects material divergence between early-stage capital-progression mechanics and the current valuations commanded by later-stage peers in a competitive frontier-biotech market. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next financing round, clinical and regulatory progress relative to peer cohorts, and the extent to which the peer set—tilted toward Series B and later-stage fundings—reflects Orchid's actual risk profile.
